task.title: Add Change Control Compiler plugin
task.request: Create a plugin that compiles vague requests into enforceable contracts before implementation.
task.problem_statement: Agents can drift from vague requests unless scope, forbidden areas, verification, and stop conditions are explicit before edits.

goal: Create a Change Control Compiler plugin that validates scope contracts and detects modified files outside allowed areas before final response.
success_criteria: The contract validator accepts this valid example.
The drift command checks git status against allowed and forbidden paths.
The plugin documents Codex, Copilot, Claude, hooks, and CI usage.

scope.summary: Add a bounded plugin with docs, skill, schema, templates, examples, hooks, CI example, and validation script.

non_goals: Do not create a desktop app.
Do not modify unrelated plugin behavior.
expected_behavior: The plugin blocks implementation until a valid contract exists and flags modified files outside allowed scope.
current_behavior: No Change Control Compiler plugin exists in the repository.
test_requirements: Validator accepts the valid contract example.
Validator rejects the invalid contract example.
Drift check passes in a clean git repository.
stop_conditions: Stop if implementation requires external dependencies.
Stop if plugin metadata requires unsupported manifest fields.
Stop if drift detection cannot be implemented with git status --short.
implementation_plan: Add skill templates, schema, examples, and script.
final_acceptance_checklist: Drift test covers clean, allowed, forbidden, and out-of-scope changes.
